name: Bloom Filter with Counting Variant
description: Implement a Bloom filter and a counting Bloom filter across two modules. Module hash_utils.py must define: HashFn dataclass with fields seed (int) and a __call__(item: bytes) -> int method that returns a non-negative integer hash; make_hashes(k: int, seeds: list[int]) -> list[HashFn] returns k independent hash functions (deterministic, seeded). Module bloom.py must import from hash_utils.py and implement: BloomFilter class with constructor BloomFilter(size: int, num_hashes: int, seeds: list[int] | None = None), methods add(item: str), contains(item: str) -> bool, __len__() returns count of distinct adds (allowing approximate over-count is OK; use a separate add counter), and approximate_fill_ratio() -> float (fraction of bits set); CountingBloomFilter with same interface plus remove(item: str) -> bool that decrements counters and returns True if any counter was non-zero.
signature:
# hash_utils.py
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class HashFn:
    seed: int
    def __call__(self, item: bytes) -> int: ...

def make_hashes(k: int, seeds: list[int]) -> list[HashFn]: ...

# bloom.py
from hash_utils import HashFn, make_hashes

class BloomFilter:
    def __init__(self, size: int, num_hashes: int, seeds: list[int] | None = None): ...
    def add(self, item: str) -> None: ...
    def contains(self, item: str) -> bool: ...
    def __len__(self) -> int: ...
    def approximate_fill_ratio(self) -> float: ...

class CountingBloomFilter(BloomFilter):
    def remove(self, item: str) -> bool: ...
tests:
def test_basic_add_contains():
    bf = BloomFilter(size=128, num_hashes=3)
    bf.add('hello')
    assert bf.contains('hello') is True

def test_missing_item():
    bf = BloomFilter(size=2048, num_hashes=5)
    bf.add('x')
    assert bf.contains('definitely_not_added_xyz_unique') is False

def test_deterministic():
    bf1 = BloomFilter(size=128, num_hashes=3, seeds=[1,2,3])
    bf2 = BloomFilter(size=128, num_hashes=3, seeds=[1,2,3])
    bf1.add('hello')
    bf2.add('hello')
    assert bf1.contains('hello') == bf2.contains('hello')

def test_len_grows():
    bf = BloomFilter(size=128, num_hashes=3)
    assert len(bf) == 0
    bf.add('a')
    bf.add('b')
    assert len(bf) == 2

def test_counting_remove():
    cbf = CountingBloomFilter(size=128, num_hashes=3)
    cbf.add('hello')
    assert cbf.contains('hello') is True
    assert cbf.remove('hello') is True
    assert cbf.contains('hello') is False

def test_counting_double_add():
    cbf = CountingBloomFilter(size=128, num_hashes=3)
    cbf.add('x')
    cbf.add('x')
    cbf.remove('x')
    assert cbf.contains('x') is True
    cbf.remove('x')
    assert cbf.contains('x') is False

def test_make_hashes_count():
    hs = make_hashes(5, [10, 20, 30, 40, 50])
    assert len(hs) == 5
    assert all(callable(h) for h in hs)
constraints: Hash functions must be deterministic given the same seed and input
BloomFilter has no false negatives (added items always return True for contains)
Default seeds (when None) should be derived deterministically from num_hashes
remove returns False only when the item's positions all have zero counters
